system.description:
A member of a bizarre class of entertainers and storytellers prized by the aristocratic city dwellers. Bards are widely accepted as blackmailers, spies, and even assassins who lead double lives while performing their trade.

Language Learning: Bards learn new languages as they advance in level. At every even numbered level above 3rd, the player may choose an additional language

Fascination: By playing music and singing, the bard can fascinate subjects in a 30' radius. May be used once per day per level. Up to 2 HD of creatures per level of the bard are affected

Morale Boost: Song provides +2 bonus to morale, 'to hit' rolls, and damage rolls to other allies while the bard is performing

Enemy Demoralization: Same song causes enemies within hearing range to incur a -2 penalty to morale, 'to hit' rolls, and damage rolls

Charm: The bard can charm creatures within range with his music

Manor Establishment: A bard can establish a manor and attract 2d6 apprentice bards of 1st level to study under the bard's tutelage

Lore Knowledge: From 2nd level onwards, a bard has a 2-in-6 chance of knowing lore pertaining to monsters, history, or heroes

Poison Expertise: Victims of poisoning by a bard suffer a -2 penalty to the saving throw, increasing with level (+1 for each 4 levels)

class.armor: Leather armor or chainmail (not plate mail or shields)
class.weapons: All missile weapons and all one-handed melee weapons
